summary: Hotz uses an extended marshmallow test metaphor to argue that society faces a collective action problem where individual incentives undermine group outcomes. He connects post-election politics to economic anxiety, arguing that wokeism appeals to those who haven't exceeded their parents economically as a form of 'moral superiority' cope. He laments that wealth redistribution is necessary but trusts neither government nor crypto to do it fairly. The post reflects his growing isolation from hustle culture and crypto while defending his long-term commitment to comma.ai and tinygrad.
key_points: Society fails collective action problems because we've abandoned shame-based enforcement mechanisms
Wokeism is a psychological coping mechanism for downward economic mobility - 'sour grapes' dressed up as moral superiority
The middle class is the only productive class but bears the burden of wealth extraction because the poor have nothing to take and the rich protect themselves
Wealth redistribution is needed but government and crypto are both untrustworthy mechanisms for it
Money is just a map of the territory - changing the map (inflation, redistribution) doesn't change underlying productive capacity
money_quotes: You need mechanisms like shame to pass, and our culture has done away with them.
Wokeism appeals to people who have not exceeded their parents economically.
In order to justify that they have moved up in the world, they cling to a cult of moral superiority.
In a fair economy, Americans have expectations exceeding their market value. They don't see a way to meet these expectations, so they reject the system.
Basically everything screws the middle class, the only class that produces value in society.
I guess I'm basically saying we have to redistribute the wealth, however, the government is the absolute last group of people I'd ever trust to do this.
Money isn't real! It's not the territory, it's the map! And changing the map doesn't change the territory, it just makes the map shittier.
I wish there was still a retreat in academia, I don't want to participate in this shit.
key_insight: Society's collective action problems stem from misaligned incentives and the erosion of shame, while proposed solutions like government redistribution or crypto only make the map worse without changing the underlying territory.
